creator: n@nectar.com
text:
On 26 April 1999 at 20:02, Mikhail Teterin <mi@misha.cisco.com> wrote:
> 	When doing the calling the krb5's
> 		/usr/local/bin/rsh the_other_side
> 	it duly fails to establish kerberos style session and warns about
> 	"trying normal rlogin (/usr/bin/rlogin)"
> 
> 	Then, something goes wrong becaus /usr/bin/rlogin just reports
> 	its usage information.
> 
> 	This may have something to do with rlogin(1) itself, and how detects
> 	its own argv[0].

It is a bug in the Kerberos5 rsh... it doesn't expect to be invoked as
rsh, but only as rlogin.  If you use rlogin, it will behave correctly.

I'll fix it in the port.

Jacques Vidrine / n@nectar.com / nectar@FreeBSD.org
